7.text: Based on the latest data, Wheat prices are projected to rise by 0.2% to reach Rs.2,508/quintal over the next week (historical error margin: ±1.5%). This movement is primarily driven by short-term momentum and recent market activity. With 3 months until the next wheat harvest, the market is in a lean-supply inter-seasonal phase where prices typically firm up as old-crop stocks diminish and fresh arrivals are limited. With prices near or below the MSP of Rs.2,600/quintal, government procurement is expected to intensify, providing strong price support and limiting further downside in the coming weeks. Our AI analysis highlights that price-to-MSP ratio (floor support), combined with price premium/discount vs. government MSP, are the strongest predictors behind this forecast. Furthermore, Month are confirming the overall bullish sentiment seen across historical patterns in mandi data. Please note that short-term projections (7-day) can be volatile and are easily swayed by day-to-day fluctuations in local mandi arrivals and regional weather.

7.text: Based on the latest data, Paddy prices are projected to rise by 5.2% to reach Rs.2,930/quintal over the next week (historical error margin: ±1.4%). This movement is primarily driven by short-term momentum and recent market activity. Currently in the paddy sowing window (June, July), market supply is thinning as the previous harvest stock depletes - this seasonal tightening supports the upward price trajectory. The current price of Rs.2,787 sits only 9.3% above the government's MSP of Rs.2,550/quintal for 2026, acting as a structural price floor - the market is unlikely to fall significantly below MSP as government procurement agencies step in. Our AI analysis highlights that Arr Roll Std 14, combined with Roll Max 90, are the strongest predictors behind this forecast. Furthermore, Arr Roll Mean 14 are confirming the overall bullish sentiment seen across historical patterns in mandi data. Please note that short-term projections (7-day) can be volatile and are easily swayed by day-to-day fluctuations in local mandi arrivals and regional weather.

7.text: Based on the latest data, Mustard prices are projected to rise by 2.2% to reach Rs.7,151/quintal over the next week (historical error margin: ±1.9%). This movement is primarily driven by short-term momentum and recent market activity. With 3 months until the next mustard harvest, the market is in a lean-supply inter-seasonal phase where prices typically firm up as old-crop stocks diminish and fresh arrivals are limited. Trading at a 13% premium to the 2026 MSP of Rs.6,200/quintal, prices reflect strong private demand and limited export competition while the MSP provides a comfortable downside buffer. Our AI analysis highlights that price premium/discount vs. government MSP, combined with annual seasonality (time of year), are the strongest predictors behind this forecast. Furthermore, short-term inter-mandi price spread are confirming the overall bullish sentiment seen across historical patterns in mandi data. Please note that short-term projections (7-day) can be volatile and are easily swayed by day-to-day fluctuations in local mandi arrivals and regional weather.
